questions.question: Define a class and an object in Java, highlighting their differences.
keyPoints: A class is a blueprint or template that defines the state and behavior of objects.
An object is an instance of a class, created using the new keyword.
Each object has its own set of attributes (fields) and methods.

questions.question: Explain the general form of a Java class, including its components and their purposes.
keyPoints: The class keyword is used to define a class in Java.
Fields (data members) are used to store the state of an object.
Methods (functions) are used to define the behavior of an object.
Objects are created using the new keyword and have their own set of attributes and methods.
The dot operator is used to access and modify the members of an object.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the use of instance variables and local variables in a Java class.
keyPoints: Instance variables are defined inside a class but outside any method and are associated with an object.
Local variables are defined inside a method and are only accessible within that method.
Instance variables are initialized with default values, while local variables must be explicitly initialized.
Instance variables can be accessed using the dot operator, while local variables are accessed directly.

questions.question: Explain the concept of method overloading in Java, including its benefits and examples.
keyPoints: Method overloading is the process of defining multiple methods with the same name but different parameters.
Method overloading allows for more flexibility and readability in programming.
The method to be invoked is determined by the number and types of arguments passed to it.
Method overloading can be used to perform different operations based on the input parameters.

questions.question: Write a simple Java program to demonstrate the use of constructors and the this keyword.
keyPoints: A constructor is a special method that is used to initialize objects when they are created.
The this keyword is used to refer to the current object and can be used to access its members.
The program should include a class with a constructor and a method that uses the this keyword.
The program should create an object and invoke the method to demonstrate the use of the this keyword.
